mnemonics.title: Coproc Array Index Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Think of coprocess file descriptors like array indices: 0 is for 'OUT' (reading output from coproc), 1 is for 'IN' (writing input to coproc). Remember: Output comes OUT through index 0.

mnemonics.title: Remembering Variables Set by Coproc
mnemonics.content: NAME_PID: Process ID of the NAMEd coprocess. NAME: Array with [0]=read, [1]=write. Think: PID = Process Identifier, Array = File Descriptors.

mnemonics.title: Communication Flow
mnemonics.content: Write to coproc → Use write end (index 1) → '1' looks like 'I' for Input. Read from coproc → Use read end (index 0) → '0' looks like 'O' for Output.

acronyms.expansion: First In First Out - a named pipe used for communication
acronyms.usage: FIFOs in coprocesses allow persistent communication channels with meaningful names, like /tmp/myapp_input for input and /tmp/myapp_output for results.
Coprocesses are one form of IPC in Unix, providing bidirectional pipe-based communication between parent shell and child process.
When write end of coproc is closed (exec NAME[1]>&-), the coprocess receives EOF on stdin, signaling no more input is coming.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine two water pipes: one flowing FROM coproc (index 0/read) TO shell, and one flowing FROM shell (index 1/write) TO coproc. Both pipes work simultaneously - full duplex.

A table with two rows: Shell FD table (stdin=0, stdout=1, stderr=2, plus coproc FDs) and Coproc FD table (its stdin, stdout). Arrow from shell FD[n] → coproc stdin; Arrow from coproc stdout → shell FD[m].
Show a pipe with a 'bucket' in the middle. Without buffering control, the bucket fills and blocks. Label one 'buffered' (slow leak) and one 'unbuffered' (open gate).

analogies.analogy: A background job (&) is like a radio station broadcasting (you can only listen). A coprocess is like a walkie-talkie - you can both send and receive messages.
Think of NAME[0] as the 'output door' from the coprocess (what comes OUT to you), and NAME[1] as the 'input door' to the coprocess (what you send IN). 0=OUT, 1=IN.

Regular pipes are like ordering food at a restaurant - each interaction spawns a new 'cook'. Coprocesses are like having your own personal chef - one process, multiple interactions, remember your preferences (state).
